questions.question: Explain the idea of self-similarity in fractals.
questions.answer: Self-similarity means smaller parts of the figure resemble the whole figure.

questions.question: How is the Sierpinski Triangle constructed?
questions.answer: It is constructed by joining the midpoints of a triangle and removing the central triangle repeatedly.

questions.question: What happens to the number of triangles in the Sierpinski Triangle after each step?
questions.answer: The number of triangles increases by a factor of 3 at each step.

questions.question: Describe the process of creating the Sierpinski Carpet.
questions.answer: Divide a square into nine equal squares and remove the central square repeatedly.

questions.question: What happens to the number of squares in the Sierpinski Carpet after each step?
questions.answer: The number of squares increases by a factor of 8 at each step.

questions.question: Explain how the Koch Snowflake is formed.
questions.answer: Each side of an equilateral triangle is divided into three parts and a smaller triangle is constructed on the middle segment.

questions.question: What happens to the perimeter of the Koch Snowflake after each iteration?
questions.answer: The perimeter increases because new segments are added at each step.

questions.question: Explain why fractals are useful in modelling natural objects.
questions.answer: Many natural patterns such as mountains and clouds show fractal-like structures.

questions.question: What is the difference between a prism and a pyramid?
questions.answer: A prism has two identical bases while a pyramid has one base and triangular faces meeting at a vertex.

questions.question: How are faces, edges and vertices related in a solid?
questions.answer: Faces are surfaces, edges join faces, and vertices are points where edges meet.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of nets of solids.
questions.answer: Nets help visualize and construct three-dimensional solids from flat shapes.

questions.question: Why are nets helpful in solving shortest path problems on cubes?
questions.answer: Unfolding the cube into a net converts surface paths into straight lines.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of projection in geometry.
questions.answer: Projection represents a three-dimensional object on a two-dimensional plane from a specific direction.

questions.question: Why are projections used in engineering drawings?
questions.answer: They help represent the dimensions and shape of objects clearly.

questions.question: Explain the difference between front view and top view.
questions.answer: Front view shows height and width while top view shows width and depth.

questions.question: Why do different objects sometimes have identical projections?
questions.answer: Projection removes depth information so different objects may appear the same.

questions.question: Explain how shadows relate to projections.
questions.answer: A shadow is a projection formed when light falls on an object.

questions.question: What is the purpose of isometric drawings?
questions.answer: They represent three-dimensional objects clearly on a two-dimensional surface.

questions.question: How does an isometric grid help in drawing solids?
questions.answer: It provides three equal directions to represent length, width and height.

questions.question: Why does a cube appear as a hexagon in isometric projection?
questions.answer: Because three faces are visible simultaneously from the viewing angle.

questions.question: Explain how cube stacking helps understand projections.
questions.answer: Stacking cubes shows how different arrangements appear from different viewing directions.

questions.question: Why is visualization important in geometry?
questions.answer: Visualization helps understand spatial relationships between shapes.

questions.question: Explain recursive construction in fractal geometry.
questions.answer: Recursive construction repeats the same rule multiple times to create complex patterns.

questions.question: How do fractals combine mathematics and art?
questions.answer: Fractals create visually appealing patterns using mathematical rules.

questions.question: What is the main idea behind geometric visualization?
questions.answer: It involves understanding and representing shapes and objects in space.
